frameworks.name: β-VAE (disentangled deep generative model)
frameworks.one_line: self-supervised model factorising sensory input into interpretable latent factors

frameworks.name: Active Appearance Model
frameworks.one_line: handcrafted state-of-the-art face perception model used as baseline

frameworks.name: deep supervised classifier
frameworks.one_line: category-trained deep networks used as alternative baseline for IT coding

frameworks.name: ventral visual stream coding
frameworks.one_line: framing learning objective of macaque face-patch responses

core_question: What learning objective best explains how single IT face-patch neurons encode faces?
key_assumptions: single-neuron IT responses reflect a learned representation of face features
interpretable latent factors of face variation exist (e.g., gender, age)
disentangled generative representations are a candidate brain objective
manipulations: model class compared (β-VAE vs. AAM vs. deep classifiers)
varying which latent units are read out for reconstruction
matching model factors to single-unit response profiles
key_findings: β-VAE latent factors correspond strongly to single IT face-patch neurons
correspondence exceeds AAM and supervised deep-classifier baselines
novel face images can be reconstructed from a small number of IT cells
interpretations: optimising disentanglement yields representations resembling single-unit IT codes
disentangling is a plausible learning objective for the visual brain
